skill.name: emaildeliverabilityskill
skill.description: Plan, review, audit, and improve email deliverability work focused on inbox placement, authentication, reputation, complaints, bounces, and remediation workflows.
skill.summary: Guides safe email deliverability audits, triage, warmup plans, and remediation runbooks.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged four high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but each referenced line is ordinary email deliverability guidance or metadata. I found no scripts, network calls, credential access, external commands, destructive actions, or prompt injection attempts in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain skill descriptions, deliverability terminology, and risk-gate language. They do not contain cryptographic code, weak hash usage, secret handling, or executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files are Markdown and YAML guidance files with no code execution path. The cited lines do not mention or invoke weak cryptographic algorithms.

content.value_statement: Email deliverability problems often mix authentication, reputation, list quality, and provider issues. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code separate causes and produce approval-gated remediation plans.
actual_capabilities: Classifies deliverability problems by authentication, bounce, complaint, blocklist, throttling, or engagement signals.
Guides reviews of SPF, DKIM, DMARC, tracking domains, bounce domains, and alignment.
Builds staged remediation plans with monitoring cadence, stop thresholds, and human owners.
Reviews list acquisition, consent, suppression, imports, frequency, and reactivation risks.
Produces audits, triage notes, warmup schedules, suppression reviews, monitoring plans, and runbooks.
limitations: Does not execute DNS changes, imports, suppression edits, sends, or production automation changes.
Requires accurate sender data, provider reports, DNS records, and campaign context for reliable recommendations.
Does not guarantee inbox placement or provider acceptance.
Does not replace legal review for consent, unsubscribe, or complaint handling obligations.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Complaint Spike
use_cases.description: Segment complaint and bounce data, identify likely sources, and recommend pause or remediation thresholds.

use_cases.title: Review Authentication Before Launch
use_cases.description: Check SPF, DKIM, DMARC, bounce domains, and tracking alignment before a new sending domain ramps up.

use_cases.title: Build a Reputation Recovery Plan
use_cases.description: Create a staged recovery plan after spam-folder placement, throttling, blocklist issues, or engagement decline.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Deliverability Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to classify my email deliverability issue. I have noticed [problem], starting on [date], for [domain or campaign]. Ask for missing evidence before recommending changes.

prompt_templates.title: DNS Authentication Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my SPF, DKIM, DMARC, bounce domain, tracking domain, and alignment setup. Separate missing records from alignment failures and list approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Warmup and Ramp Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a new or recovering sender
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a warmup plan for [domain], current volume [volume], target volume [volume], and known risks [risks]. Include stop, slow, and continue thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Full Remediation Runbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced response to multiple reputation signals
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a remediation runbook from provider reports, bounce trends, complaint trends, list sources, recent imports, and campaign history. Include owners, monitoring cadence, and approval gates.

output_examples.input: Review a new sending domain before launch.
output_examples.output: A readiness audit that separates SPF, DKIM, DMARC, bounce domain, and tracking alignment checks, then lists required approvals before production use.

output_examples.input: Investigate a sudden rise in spam-folder placement.
output_examples.output: A triage note that compares authentication, complaint, bounce, provider, list source, and engagement signals before recommending staged recovery actions.

output_examples.input: Plan a ramp after a provider migration.
output_examples.output: A warmup schedule with daily volume guidance, monitoring thresholds, risk gates, and pause criteria for high-risk signals.

best_practices: Provide domain, provider, campaign, segment, and time-window data before asking for recommendations.
Separate analysis from execution for DNS, suppression, imports, sends, and automation changes.
Use complaint, bounce, unsubscribe, engagement, and provider data together when judging reputation risk.
anti_patterns: Do not treat all deliverability problems as content problems without checking authentication and list quality.
Do not continue high-risk sends while complaint, bounce, or blocklist signals are worsening.
Do not change DNS, suppression, imports, or production automation without explicit human approval.

faq.question: Can this skill make DNS changes for me?
faq.answer: No. It can review records and recommend changes, but DNS edits require explicit human approval and separate execution.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee inbox placement?
faq.answer: No. It helps identify risks and plan remediation, but mailbox providers control filtering decisions.

faq.question: What data should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide sending domains, provider, DNS records, bounce trends, complaint trends, campaign details, and recent list changes.

faq.question: Can it help with DMARC alignment?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides SPF, DKIM, DMARC, bounce domain, and tracking domain checks with separate alignment analysis.

faq.question: Is this useful during a complaint spike?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps segment the issue by source, campaign, provider, and time window before recommending controls.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code and focuses on planning, review, and audit workflows.
